info.title: 3D Manufacturing Operations Management Web Services
info.description: REST Services for integrating the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform with ERP systems through the 3DEXPERIENCE Manufacturing Operations Management Services.

get.description: Gets the details of a Receiving Order including parameters of the order as well as the status of its operations.

post.description:
Creates a new Receiving Order based on the request body object.

## Object Structure

Request body object JSON has a hierarchical form consisting of several sections:

1.  Order header

2.  Order lines

3.  Order containerization – this section is only available and mandatory for the order type 17 - ASN Inbound Delivery Order

If an imported order has the status New, Released or Held, it is possible to replace it with a new order with the same ```OrderNo``` and ```OrderType```. For orders that currently have a different status, if you try to import the same order, an error will be returned.

The Unit of Measure (UOM) in an order line should be consistent with the manufacturing item's unit or product’s UOM:
- **Continuous Manufactured Material**: Use the unit defined for the item (e.g., KILOGRAM, METER, METER2, or METER3).
- **Continuous Provided Material**: Use the default unit based on its magnitude:
  - KILOGRAM for mass
  - METER for length
  - METER2 for area
  - METER3 for volume
- **All other item types**: Use EA (Each).
- **Product that has been created in the MODM app**: Use the UOM defined for the product.

All translated content during import should be created in the language in which the endpoint is called (a user context needs to be available).

***note:** Accepted order types are 16 – Purchase Order and 17 - ASN Inbound Delivery Order. All others will be rejected with an internal server error.*

***note:** Supplier (partner) should be created before an order is imported. The imported order will have status 1-New.*

***note:** At least one of the following parameters must be provided: ManufacturingItemId, ManufacturingItemEin, or ProductNo. If both ManufacturingItemId and ManufacturingItemEin are provided, ManufacturingItemId will be used to retrieve the manufacturing item's properties.*

***note:** Either the ToWarehouseId, or both ToFacility and ToWarehouse must be provided. If all three are provided, ToWarehouseId is used.*

get.description: Gets the details of a Issue Order including parameters of the order as well as the status of its operations.

post.description:
Creates a new Issue Order based on the request body object.

## Object Structure

Request body object JSON has a hierarchical form consisting of several sections:

1.  Order header

2.  Order lines

If an imported order has the status New, Released or Held, it is possible to replace it with a new order with the same ```OrderNo``` and ```OrderType```. For orders that currently have a different status, if you try to import the same order, an error will be returned. 

The Unit of Measure (UOM) in an order line should be consistent with the manufacturing item's unit or product’s UOM:
- **Continuous Manufactured Material**: Use the unit defined for the item (e.g., KILOGRAM, METER, METER2, or METER3).
- **Continuous Provided Material**: Use the default unit based on its magnitude:
  - KILOGRAM for mass
  - METER for length
  - METER2 for area
  - METER3 for volume
- **All other item types**: Use EA (Each).
- **Product that has been created in the MODM app**: Use the UOM defined for the product.

All translated content during import should be created in the language in which the endpoint is called (a user context needs to be available).

***note:** Accepted order types are 7 - Delivery Order and 24 – Production Picking Order. All others will be rejected with an internal server error.*

***note:** Customer (partner) should be created before an order is imported. The imported order will have status 1-New.*

***note:** At least one of the following parameters must be provided: ManufacturingItemId, ManufacturingItemEin, or ProductNo. If both ManufacturingItemId and ManufacturingItemEin are provided, ManufacturingItemId will be used to retrieve the manufacturing item's properties.*

***note:** Either FromWarehouseId, or both FromFacility and FromWarehouse must be provided. If all are provided, FromWarehouseId is used. Similarly, either ToWarehouseId, or both ToFacility and ToWarehouse must be provided. If all are provided, ToWarehouseId is used.*

get.description:
Gets the details of warehouse inventory change events for a given completed or closed order.

In some cases, when one or more properties have changed for existing inventory (for example, when inventory is moved between two warehouses, or the inventory status is changed), the details list in the response body object JSON will contain two objects for the inventory: one with its previous properties and quantity less than zero, and one with its new properties and quantity greater than zero.

Gets the details of warehouse inventory change events for a given inventory history identifier.

In some cases, when one or more properties have changed for existing inventory (for example, when inventory is moved between two warehouses, or the inventory status is changed, or in some cases during counting reconciliation), the details list in the response body object JSON will contain two objects for the inventory: one with its previous properties and quantity less than zero, and one with its new properties and quantity greater than zero.

post.description:
The endpoint will be used to send readings from asset and persist such a series as data collect readings within the context of a single operation in the 3DEXPERIENCE MOM Service database for the purpose of monitoring of production equipment behavior. The asset will pass the data in the JSON format. The corresponding input JSON variable will consist of two logical parts:

1. Properties that allow the identification of the resource assetRef section or assetSerial.
2. The collection of the asset readings, each described up to name, data type, values, and timestamp.

Upon endpoint call with these specified data, the MOM Service will first identify the asset based on the provided assetRef or assetSerial. If the asset is successfully identified, then the 3DEXPERIENCE MOM Service will try to find the right production operation, and serialized manufacturing item. For this purpose, the production operation must be started and the asset (resource) usage confirmed in the operation for the serialized manufacturing item by the Operator.

Properties that allow the identification of the resource assetRef or assetSerial, where:
- If an asset has been defined in the process - it can be referenced via section assetRef or assetSerial. Serial Number is the unique serial number defined in 3DSpace for this resource.
- If resource classes have been defined in the process, it is necessary to use an asset linked to this class and added under the same work center as the operation.

The found triple of objects (confirmed asset, started operation, serialized manufacturing item) needs to be only one. Otherwise (if the asset cannot be identified, or multiple such data have been found) then exceptions will be raised. Once the triple is successfully identified, the MOM Service tries to match the asset readings (by the name sent in the assetReadings section) to the data collects rows in the operation. When the appropriate data collects rows are identified, then the system will persist the asset readings as data collect readings for the identified serialized manufacturing item. At this point, the operator sees a message about the new data and the part of the screen corresponding to the collected reading data (Data Collect) refreshes automatically. When at least one asset reading are not mapped to the operation data collect properly then exceptions will be raised.

get.description: Gets the details of a print request based on the provided ID.
Generates a CSRF token required for web services using the verb PUT, PATCH, POST, or DELETE. The token is valid for the session. After a newlogin the token is no longer valid.

success-order-post-base.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.

success-production-get.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.

CompletedQuantity.description: The quantity of the order that has been completed.

OrderProgress.description: The percentage process of an order.

OperationSeqNo.description: The sequence number of an operation.

OperationProgress.description: The percentage process of an operation.

success-warehouse-get.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.

OrderLineNo.description: The number of an order line.

ProductNo.description: The product for an order line.

ProductRevision.description: The product revision for an order line.

ManufacturingItemId.description: The physical ID of the Manufacturing Item.

ManufacturingItemEin.description: The EIN (Part Number) of the Manufacturing Item.

OrderedQuantity.description: The ordered quantity of an order line.

CompletedQuantity.description: The quantity of an order line that has been completed.

OrderLineStatus.description: The status of an order line.

UOM.description:
Unit or UOM is used depending on how the manufacturing item or product was identified.

- Unit: ManufacturingItemId or ManufacturingItemEin
- UOM: ProductNo and ProductRevision

error-400-get.description: The response data when the request fails with a 400 error.

Message.example: The format of provided OrderKey is not correct.

error-400-post.description: The response data when the request fails with a 400 error.

Message.example: Request body is empty or incorrect JSON format.

error-403.description: The response data when the request fails with a 403 error.

Message.description: An error message describing the user is not allowed to make the request.
Message.example: The currently logged in employee does not have the necessary roles to access the provided Web Service.

error-403-camel.description: The response data when the request fails with a 403 error.

message.description: An error message describing the user is not allowed to make the request.
message.example: The currently logged in employee does not have the necessary roles to access the provided Web Service.

error-404.description: The response data when the request fails with a 404 error.

Message.description: An error message describing the specified entity was not found.

error-404-camel.description: The response data when the request fails with a 404 error.

message.description: An error message describing the specified entity was not found.

Message.example: Provided OrderNo WO01 does not exist for OrderType 10001.
Provided OrderNo RCV102 does not exist for OrderType 16.
Provided OrderNo PPO01 does not exist for OrderType 24.

error-500.description: The response data when the request fails with a 500 error.

Message.description: An error message describing the error in system processing. It may be generic or precise depending on the nature of the error.
Message.example: An error occurred while processing this request.

error-500-camel.description: The response data when the request fails with a 500 error.

message.description: An error message describing the error in system processing. It may be generic or precise depending on the nature of the error.
message.example: An error occurred while processing this request.

Message.example: Tool 'Tool44_MW' has already different Resource Class assigned 'Tools44'

error-501.description: The response data when the request fails with a 501 error.

Message.description: An error message describing the error in system configuration.
Message.example: The system is not configured for this request.

error-501-camel.description: The response data when the request fails with a 501 error.

message.description: An error message describing the error in system configuration.
message.example: The system is not configured for this request.

Order.description:
```Facility``` must exist in the target system when the order is imported. Otherwise, the request will fail with an internal server error.

Only orders of type 10001 are accepted.

All string values, have to be given in the quotation marks to be correctly read.

ManufacturingItemId.description: The physical ID of the Manufacturing Item.

ManufacturingItemEin.description: The EIN (Part Number) of the Manufacturing Item.

UOM.description:
Unit or UOM is used depending on how the manufacturing item or product was identified.

- Unit: ManufacturingItemId or ManufacturingItemEin
- UOM: ProductNo and ProductRevision

ToFacility.description: Facility where inventory is delivered and staged

ToWarehouse.description: Warehouse where inventory is delivered and staged

ToWarehouseLocation.description: Warehouse location where inventory is delivered and staged

UnderTolerance.description: Tolerance by which completed quantity can be lower than ordered quantity. 0.1 means a tolerance of 10% against the requested quantity.

OverTolerance.description: Tolerance by which completed quantity can exceed ordered quantity. 0.1 means a tolerance of 10% against the requested quantity.

ToWarehouseId.description: The physical ID of the Warehouse.

ToWarehouseLocationId.description: The physical ID of the Location.

OrderLineNo.description: The name of the order line from the OrderLines collection.

ParentContainer.description: The name of the parent container.

LotNo.description: The product lot. Required if lot tracked.

SerialNo.description: The product serial number. Required if serial tracked.

ToWarehouseLocation.description: Warehouse location where inventory is received

OrderType.description:
Accepted types of orders

* `16` - Purchase Order
* `17` - ASN Inbound Delivery Order

FromFacility.description: Facility from which inventory is picked

FromWarehouse.description: Warehouse from which inventory is picked

FromZone.description: Zone from which inventory is picked

FromWarehouseLocation.description: Warehouse location from which inventory is picked

FromWarehouseId.description: The physical ID of the Warehouse.

FromWarehouseLocationId.description: The physical ID of the Location.

OrderType.description:
Accepted types of orders

* `7` - Delivery Order
* `24` - Production Picking Order

Sequence.description: The order in which the component is used.

Revision.description: A versioning identifier. Product revision that is created does not get any updates.

uom.description:
Unit or UOM is used depending on how the manufacturing item or product was identified.

- Unit: ManufacturingItemId or ManufacturingItemEin
- UOM: ProductNo and ProductRevision

manufacturingItemId.description: The physical ID of the Manufacturing Item.

manufacturingItemEin.description: The EIN (Part Number) of the Manufacturing Item.

OrderType.description:
Defines the type of order.

Only orders of type 10001 are accepted.

Rank.description: Rank of the order (ex. identifier of a final good for which the part is built)

Project.description: Project of the order (ex. type/model of a final good for which the part is built)

Facility.description:
The facility in which the order is executed.

```Facility``` must exist in the target system when the order is imported. Otherwise, the request will fail with an internal server error.

Serials.description: List of serial numbers that will be built within an order

Product.description: The Product is always created as serial-tracked and non-lot-tracked. Each time a product is assigned to an order, it is checked whether the product's facility matches the order's facility. If the product does not have a linked facility, the link is created.

OrderRouting.description:
Order routing (dependencies between operations)

When you import orders with cycled routing, their operations are not visible on the task list. Cycled routing happens when you route operation A to operation B and the other way round.

DueDate.description: Date for which the order is due

Components.description:
The component usage under the operation.
If a component does not exist, it will be created.

Resources.description:
The resource usage requests under the operation.
If a resource does not exist, it will be created – the created tool should be of "Tool" type.

Characteristics.description:
The characteritics of the operation.
If a characteristic does not exist, it will be created. Both value and attribute characteristics can be assigned. For value characteristics limits (specification, control, coherence) can be defined. A minimum of two limits must be defined. For attribute characteristics, a list of attributes can be provided with an indication of conformity.

WorkInstructions.description: The work instructions of the operation. If a work instruction does not exist, it will be created.

WorkCenter.description: The location of executing the operation. If the given work center does not exist, it will be created (within a facility of the order). If there exists a work center with the same name but in a different facility the request will fail with an internal server error.

Sequence.description: The sequence of the step within the operation.

Name.description: The name of the step in the operation

Components.description:
The component usage under the step.
If a component does not exist, it will be created.

Resources.description:
The resource usage requests under the step.
If a resource does not exist, it will be created – the created tool should be of "Tool" type.

Characteristics.description:
The characteritics of the step.
If a characteristic does not exist, it will be created. Both value and attribute characteristics can be assigned. For value characteristics limits (specification, control, coherence) can be defined. A minimum of two limits must be defined. For attribute characteristics, a list of attributes can be provided with an indication of conformity.

WorkInstructions.description: The work instructions of the step. If a work instruction does not exist, it will be created.

Quality.description: The outcome from executing the operation.

ReasonCode.description: Reason code used for routing of a product over the order operations routing

MergeGroup.description:
Merge group attribute used to descripe parallel routings.

In the case of parallel routing, to set the correct order routing, operations merging into one need to have the same value of the additional Merge Group parameter. Parallel routing happens when an operation is completed and the succeeding parallel operations require the same status code.

Revision.description: Product revision If the Product does not exist, this revision will become the default

IsLotTracked.description: Flag to set the component tracked in lots

IsSerialTracked.description: Flag to set the component tracked in serials

Quantity.description: The quantity of a component is a total quantity of a component required to execute the whole order. So quantity for a single product unit will be equal to the ratio of a Quantity and an Order.Quantity.

Name.description: The name of the work instruction.

Text.example: Step1

Sequence.description: The order in which the work instruction is used

Type.description:
Type of characteristic.

* `INT` - Value-type characteristics
* `ATTR` - Attribute-type characteristics

UOM.description: Unit of measure code, only for value-type characteristics

LHL.description: Lower Coherence Limit, only for value-type characteristics

LSL.description: Lower Specification Limit, only for value-type characteristics

LCL.description: Lower Control Limit, only for value-type characteristics

TargetValue.description: Target Value, only for value-type characteristics

UCL.description: Upper Control Limit, only for value-type characteristics

USL.description: Upper Specification Limit, only for value-type characteristics

UHL.description: Upper Coherence Limit, only for value-type characteristics

Conf.description: Whether or not the value is configured.

success-inventory-change-get.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.

detailsCount.description: A count of items in the Details array.

warehouse.description: Warehouse where the invetory change took place.

lotNo.description: The product lot. Present if lot tracked.

serialNo.description: The product serial number. Present if serial tracked.

warehouseId.description: The physical ID of the Warehouse.

message.example: Provided ID of inventory change is not correct.
The format of provided OrderKey is not correct.
Provided ID of inventory change does not exist.
Provided orderNo RCV102 does not exist for orderType 16

name.description: Name of the header the CSRF token is applicable.

readings.description: Array of assets and their readings.

items.description: An object containing either assetRef or assetSerial and related readings.

source.description: URL source for the asset reference.

relativePath.description: Relative path to the asset resource.

assetSerial.description: Unique Serial number of the asset as defined in 3DSpace.

assetReadings.description: Array of readings related to the asset.

values.description: Array of values for the reading parameter.

message.example: The single triple: the asset, the production operation, the serialized manufacturing item has been identified and all the asset readings have been successfully mapped to data collects. The corresponding asset readings have been persisted in the MOM Service database as data collect readings concerning the identified serialized manufacturing item.
Incorrect JSON format of the payload.
User is not authorized to access this resource.
The currently logged in employee does not have the necessary roles to access the provided Web Service.

The single triple (the asset, the production operation, the serialized manufacturing item) has not been identified (either none or multiple operations found), or at least one asset reading has not been mapped to the operation data collect properly.

200-print-get.description:
The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.

* `ContainerNo` - **string** 

Container number

Example="C1"

* `ProductNo` - **string**

The product name or number

Example"MM1-13/0045"

* `ProductDesc` - **string**

The product description

Example="Product desription"

* `LotNo` - **string**

Lot number

Example="LX5667"
* `SerialNo` - **string**

Serial number

Example="S0001"

* `Quantity` - **number**

Product quantity

Example=10.5

* `Facility` - **string**

Facility

Example="F1"

* `Warehouse` - **string**

Warehouse

Example="W1"

* `Location` - **string**

Warehouse Location in format Name+Revision

Example="L11"

* `Employee` - **string**

Employee who prints label

Example="Emp01"

* `PrintType` - **string**

Type of labeling or barcode printing method used for the identifier (e.g., ContainerNo, ProductNo) of the product or container.

Possible Values: 

- "PRINT_CONTAINER"
- "PRINT_PRODUCT"

400-print-get.example: The format of provided PrintRequestID is not correct.

403-print-get.example: The currently logged in employee does not have the necessary roles to access the provided Web Service.

500-print-get.example: An error occurred while processing this request.

501-print-get.example: The system is not configured for this request.

OrderKey.name: OrderKey
OrderKey.description: The unique identifier for an order. A composite key comprised of the type and name of an order separated with a hyphen.

OrderKeyReceiving.name: OrderKey
OrderKeyReceiving.description: The unique identifier for an order. A composite key comprised of the type and name of an order separated with a hyphen.

OrderKeyIssue.name: OrderKey
OrderKeyIssue.description: The unique identifier for an order. A composite key comprised of the type and name of an order separated with a hyphen.

OrderKeyInventoryChange.name: OrderKey
OrderKeyInventoryChange.description: The unique identifier for an order. A composite key comprised of the type and name of an order separated with a hyphen.

InventoryHistoryIDInventoryChange.name: InventoryHistoryID
InventoryHistoryIDInventoryChange.description: The unique identifier for an inventory history record.

csrf.name: X-CSRF-APRISO
csrf.description: The CSRF token which is required for update operations. Retrieve using /resources/v1/application/CSRF.

PrintRequestID.name: PrintRequestID
PrintRequestID.description: The unique identifier for a print request.
